completion: Decide fast on reversible choices—delay is more costly than a wrong turn.
Ship the smallest valuable version, then learn in the wild.
Add delight where frustration once lived.
Stabilize culture and systems before chasing the next spike.

prompt: On preventing silos in small teams
completion: Rotate responsibilities to keep empathy and awareness high.
Earn trust through consistency before designing the logo.
Break them into the smallest questions you can answer today.
Fix the thing causing the most user pain first.
Hand off outcomes, not just tasks.

prompt: Approach to maintaining clarity in scaling teams
completion: Overcommunicate when adding layers of management.
Own it, fix it, show what you learned.

prompt: Guideline for onboarding new hires fast
completion: Immerse them in the product and users before processes.
Push daily, but accept that big arcs take time.
Test with small segments before changing for everyone.
Forecast pessimistically, spend optimistically only after wins.

prompt: On converting free users to paid
completion: Make the free version valuable and the paid version irresistible.

prompt: Principle for setting technical debt policy
completion: Document it, revisit it, and pay it down before it chokes you.

prompt: Advice for maintaining momentum after launch
completion: Line up the next release before the current one ships.
Count solved problems, not just completed tasks.
Codify what works before scaling the team.
Anchor it in user outcomes, not feature lists.
Every extra step is a drop-off point—trim relentlessly.
Stack value until the price feels low.
Agree on the goal, measure the result, own the gap.
Talk to them weekly, even when things are going well.

prompt: Tip for handling multiple growth ideas
completion: Bet big on one, test the rest small.
Make it memorable, pronounceable, and true to the core use.

prompt: Advice for aligning marketing and product
completion: Promise only what you can deliver now—or soon.
Cut losses quickly, then diagnose the hiring gap.
Onboard so well they can’t imagine leaving.

prompt: Tip for expanding into adjacent markets
completion: Win a niche completely before crossing borders.
Data settles debates faster than opinions.

prompt: On balancing automation and personal touch
completion: Automate the routine, personalize the exceptional.

prompt: Advice for testing bold product ideas
completion: Prototype privately before betting publicly.
Make the mission tangible in everyday language.
Invest in infrastructure and learning before the next sprint.

prompt: Tip for preventing burnout during hypergrowth
completion: Embed recovery days into the launch cycle.
Pick the one that drives survival this quarter.
Ask questions that uncover actions, not opinions.
Exploit one before diversifying to many.
Re-scope instead of blindly extending deadlines.

prompt: Advice on turning small wins into leverage
completion: Publicize progress to attract talent and allies.

prompt: On the role of intuition in product calls
completion: Use it to frame hypotheses, then test with data.

prompt: Tip for keeping cross-functional teams in sync
completion: Share metrics and language across departments.

prompt: Guidance for setting healthy founder boundaries
completion: Protect time for thinking like you protect cash flow.
Spot slipping engagement before it shows in revenue.
Steal the why, reinvent the how.
Invest in tools that grow with ticket volume, not just headcount.
